name: Lines on the North
author: Hommil Dundreth, Merchant-Mage of Hlintar
description:
Hommil Dundreth, merchant-mage of Hlintar, versifies trade wisdom — when to buy furs, when to sell steel, and how winter turns copper into gold for those who survive it.

The poem is taught in counting-houses; Dundreth's enemies claim he hid loan-shark rates in the metre.

name: Luskan Threat to Neverwinter
author: Unknown
description:
An intelligence brief on Luskan's naval ambitions — ship counts, Hosttower meddling, and the historical pattern of winter raids on Neverwinter's harbour.

The author is unnamed; Lord Neverember's seal appears on some copies, suggesting official sponsorship and selective distribution.

name: Legends of Icewind Dale
author: Unknown
description:
Legends of Icewind Dale — Legends collected from tavern verse, elder memory, and sources no scholar will swear to in court. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1312 DR.

Each generation adds a verse; purists claim the oldest layer is true and everything since is flattery.

name: Lords' Writ
author: Ahghairon
description:
Lords' Writ — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Ahghairon is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1039 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Legacy of the Three Shields
author: Iana Robinsong of Silverymoon
description:
Legacy of the Three Shields — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Iana Robinsong of Silverymoon is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 900s DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Lore of the Coast
author: Irigoth Mmar
description:
Lore of the Coast — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Irigoth Mmar is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1223 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Life on the Rack
author: Shavryn Ilidar
description:
Life on the Rack — Devotional and doctrinal writing circulated among temples and faithful households along the trade routes. Shavryn Ilidar is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 650 DR.

Temple libraries hold the cleanest editions; roadside chapbooks may omit verses the clergy prefer not to recite aloud.

name: Lost Tales of Myth Drannor
author: Skerrit the Green
description:
Lost Tales of Myth Drannor — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Skerrit the Green is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 15th century DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Life Beneath the Sails
author: Anonymous
description:
Life Beneath the Sails — Practical notes for wayfarers — tolls, seasons, inns, and the unwritten rules of safe passage. Anonymous is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1369 DR.

Experienced caravans treat the counsel as suggestion only — but rookies who ignore it seldom return to disagree.
